PenSignature

Professional Email Signature Builder

Personal Information

Full name and job title are always shown. Toggle Department and Company.

Contact Details

Toggle which fields appear in your signature.

Profile Photo & Logo

Please upload your image to a public host (e.g. Imgur, Dropbox, or company CDN) and paste the direct image link here.

Social Media Links

Add, remove, and customize as many social or external links as you need.

Select Layout Template

Choose a pre-designed template. All templates are coded with strict table structures optimized for compatibility across email clients.

Classic Executive

Left image with a vertical color divider and neat text blocks. Best for formal corporate use.

Minimalist Clean

Single column structure without images, clean layout spacing. Super safe & highly readable.

Modern Badge

Clean double column with accent title banner style. Ideal for tech, startup, or design agencies.

Left Accent Ribbon

A thick colored left accent border running the height of the signature. Bold and modern.

Design Theme Colors

Customize colors for your signature layout icons and text elements.

Typography & Sizing

Web-safe fonts are recommended to ensure consistent rendering in all clients.

Disclaimer & Footers

Subject: New Email Signature Mockup
Copied to clipboard!

Installation Guide

  1. Click the Copy Signature button on the preview dashboard to copy the formatted signature to your clipboard.
  2. Open your Gmail and click the gear icon in the top right, then select See all settings.
  3. Scroll down to the Signature section and click Create new (or select an existing signature).
  4. Click inside the signature text area and press Ctrl+V (or Cmd+V on Mac) to paste.
  5. Scroll to the bottom of the Gmail settings page and click Save Changes.

Note: Make sure "Signature defaults" are updated to apply the new signature to new emails and replies/forwards.

  1. Click the Copy Signature button on the preview dashboard.
  2. Open Outlook and click on File > Options > Mail > Signatures....
  3. Click New to create a new signature template, and give it a name.
  4. Click in the editor box and press Ctrl+V (or Cmd+V) to paste your signature.
  5. Select your new signature under the "Choose default signature" settings for your email account, then click OK.

If some details shift, try pasting using the Keep Source Formatting option (which appears near the paste cursor in Outlook).

  1. Click the Copy Signature button to save the formatted signature.
  2. Open Mail on Mac and go to Mail > Settings (or Preferences) > Signatures.
  3. Select your email account and click the + button to add a new signature.
  4. Uncheck the checkbox that says "Always match my default message font".
  5. Paste the signature into the text box using Cmd+V.
